subject_tmpl: Daily arrest/booking log — {DATE} ({NUM} custodial arrests)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, here is the overnight arrest and booking summary for {DATE}. We logged {NUM} custodial arrests: {NUM} on local ordinance charges, {NUM} misdemeanor, and {NUM} felony. Of those, {NUM} were released on I-bond and {NUM} were held for bond court at the Maybrook courthouse. One notable booking — a subject taken into custody on a felony warrant during a traffic stop at {ADDRESS} (case {CASE}). No use-of-force entries on this shift and no injuries to arrestees or officers. The full blotter with charge codes is attached. Let me know if you'd like anything pulled before your morning staff meeting.
reply_tmpl: Thanks. Nothing further needed — just flag it for me if any of these turn into press inquiries.

subject_tmpl: Weekly arrest/booking recap — {DATE} through {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the weekly booking recap is ready. Across the seven-day period we processed {NUM} custodial arrests, up {NUM} from the prior week. DUI accounted for {NUM} of those, with a weekend cluster of {NUM} on Saturday night alone. Two subjects were held without bond pending detention hearings. We also cleared {NUM} outstanding warrants through arrests made during routine stops. Booking photos and custody dispositions are filed per the {NUM}-year ILCS retention schedule. I've attached the spreadsheet sorted by charge class. Happy to walk Tina through any of it for the council digest.

subject_tmpl: SR-1050 crash report — injury accident at {STREET} & {CROSS}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, flagging a reportable crash for your awareness ahead of any constituent calls. At approximately {TIME} on {DATE}, officers responded to a two-vehicle injury collision at {STREET} and {CROSS}. One occupant, age {AGE}, was transported to Loyola with non-life-threatening injuries; the at-fault driver was cited for failure to yield. The state SR-1050 form has been completed and submitted to IDOT, and the intersection was cleared and reopened by {TIME}. This is the {NUM}th reportable crash at that intersection this year, which keeps it on our high-frequency watch list. Report number is {CASE}. I'll have the full crash diagram available if Public Works wants it for the safety study.
reply_tmpl: Please send the crash count for that intersection to Frank — if it's trending we should get it into the capital safety review.

subject_tmpl: Monthly traffic-crash summary & high-frequency intersection memo — {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, attached is the monthly traffic-crash summary for {DATE}. Total reportable crashes (SR-1050): {NUM}, down {NUM}% from the prior month and down {NUM}% year-over-year. Injury crashes: {NUM}; property-damage-only: {NUM}; no fatalities this period. The top three high-frequency locations remain {STREET} & {CROSS} ({NUM} crashes), the {STREET} corridor near the Metra crossing ({NUM}), and {ADDRESS} ({NUM}). All forms were transmitted to IDOT within the reporting window. I've recommended a focused enforcement detail at the top intersection for next month. Let me know if you'd like the heat map slide for the board presentation.
reply_tmpl: Good — put the heat map in the deck for Tuesday and copy Frank so the engineering side is aware.

subject_tmpl: Crash report request from village insurance carrier — claim on {CASE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, our risk pool's third-party adjuster has requested a certified copy of the SR-1050 crash report for the {DATE} collision involving a village vehicle at {ADDRESS} (case {CASE}). The records unit has prepared the certified copy and the accompanying squad-car dash footage. Before I release it I wanted to confirm with your office and the village attorney that there's no litigation hold that would change how we handle it. Assuming no objection, I'll process the release under the standard crash-report fee and log it. The adjuster's contact is {PHONE}. Please advise by end of week.

subject_tmpl: Monthly citation & ordinance-fine revenue report — {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the monthly citation and ordinance-violation summary is attached for {DATE}. Officers issued {NUM} moving violations, {NUM} equipment citations, and {NUM} local ordinance tickets. Administrative-hearing adjudications totaled {NUM} cases, with {NUM} found liable and {NUM} dismissed or continued. Total fine revenue posted to the village was {AMOUNT}, which is roughly {NUM}% above the same month last year, driven mainly by the school-zone enforcement detail. A full breakdown by violation type and a comparison to the monthly target are in the attachment. Finance has the same copy for reconciliation.
reply_tmpl: Thanks. Send the revenue figure to David so it's reflected in the mid-year projection.

subject_tmpl: FW: Resident dispute over property-maintenance ordinance ticket — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, your office forwarded a complaint from {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS} disputing an ordinance-violation notice issued on {DATE} (case {CASE}). I've pulled the file. The officer documented an inoperable vehicle stored in open view and issued a {NUM}-day compliance notice before the citation; photos are in the record. The resident is entitled to contest it at the next administrative-adjudication docket on {DATE}, and I've included the hearing-request form they'll need. We followed the ordinance to the letter here, but I wanted you to have the full picture before responding to {FIRST}. Their callback number is {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Thanks for the quick turnaround. I'll let them know the hearing is the proper venue and send them the form.

subject_tmpl: FOIA #{CASE} — body-cam footage request (5-day clock started)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, we received FOIA request #{CASE} on {DATE} from {FIRST} {LAST} seeking body-worn and dash-camera footage from an officer contact at {ADDRESS} on {DATE}. The five-business-day statutory clock is running, with a response due {DATE}. The footage exists and is preserved. We anticipate redactions for third-party faces and any audio of minors under the standard exemptions, which the village attorney will review. This may be a request where we exercise the one-time five-day extension given the redaction volume. I'll keep your office posted, but wanted you aware in case the requester contacts you directly. Nothing is needed from you at this time.

subject_tmpl: Pending FOIA log — {NUM} open requests, {NUM} due Friday
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, here is the weekly snapshot of open police FOIA requests. We currently have {NUM} pending: {NUM} for written reports, {NUM} for the arrest blotter, and {NUM} for body-cam or squad video. {NUM} are due this Friday. The video requests are the time-consuming ones because of redaction review. We are on track to meet every statutory deadline this week and have not had to deny any request in full. One attorney request tied to case {CASE} may generate an appeal to the Public Access Counselor — I'll flag that separately if it does. The full tracking log is attached for the transparency file.
reply_tmpl: Thanks — this is exactly the kind of running log I want. Keep it coming weekly.

subject_tmpl: FW: Attorney FOIA for crash report & squad video — case {CASE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, an attorney representing a party in the {DATE} crash at {ADDRESS} has filed a FOIA for the SR-1050 crash report and all squad and body-camera video from the scene (case {CASE}). Because there's a likelihood of civil litigation, I've looped in the village attorney before we release anything. We will produce the records on the statutory timeline unless legal advises a specific exemption applies. The requester's representative can be reached at {PHONE}. I don't need anything from your office — just keeping you in the loop since these sometimes generate follow-up calls to the mayor.
reply_tmpl: Understood. Coordinate the release with legal and let me know the date it goes out.

subject_tmpl: Chief's monthly crime stats report — {DATE} (Part I crimes down {NUM}%)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the monthly CompStat summary for {DATE} is attached. Part I (index) crime is down {NUM}% from the prior month and down {NUM}% year-to-date. Violent-crime offenses held flat at {NUM}, while property crime fell to {NUM}, led by a {NUM}% drop in motor-vehicle theft following the {STREET} corridor detail. Two categories ticked up: retail theft (+{NUM}) concentrated at the {ADDRESS} plaza, and criminal damage to vehicles (+{NUM}) along the Washington Blvd corridor. Clearance rate for the month was {NUM}%. Arrests totaled {NUM}; calls for service, {NUM}. The CompStat slides are ready for Tuesday's board meeting. I can present in person or hand it off to you — your call.
reply_tmpl: Strong numbers. Plan to present the property-crime slides yourself — the board will have questions on the retail-theft uptick.

subject_tmpl: NIBRS submission confirmed to ISP for {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, this is a brief confirmation for the record that our monthly NIBRS crime-data submission to the Illinois State Police was accepted for the {DATE} reporting period with zero error flags. All offense reports were classified by IUCR code and validated before transmission. The accepted submission means the village remains in good standing for state and federal grant eligibility that depends on timely UCR reporting. The aggregate counts that feed the CompStat memo match the certified submission exactly. No action needed — I'm just documenting the confirmation in the compliance file and copying your office.

subject_tmpl: CompStat slides for Tuesday's village board presentation
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the CompStat deck for the {DATE} board meeting is finished and attached. The narrative this cycle is positive: total index crime down {NUM}% versus the same period last year, with the biggest gains in residential burglary (down {NUM}) and motor-vehicle theft (down {NUM}). The map slides highlight the two micro-hot-spots we're working — the {STREET} retail strip and the {ADDRESS} apartment cluster — along with the directed-patrol response. I kept it to {NUM} slides so it fits the time you have on the agenda. If you'd like, I can do a five-minute dry run with you and Tina beforehand so the talking points line up.
reply_tmpl: Let's do the dry run {DATE} at {TIME} in my office. Bring the hot-spot maps printed too.

subject_tmpl: Community alert: porch-package thefts — please share with residents
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, we've seen a cluster of porch-package thefts this week, with {NUM} reports concentrated in the neighborhoods east of {STREET}. We've drafted a community-policing advisory reminding residents to track deliveries, use parcel lockers where available, and report suspicious vehicles to the non-emergency line at {PHONE}. The bulletin is short and ready to push to the village email list and social channels — I'd appreciate your office sharing it as well, since it carries more weight coming from you. A directed patrol is already assigned to the affected blocks during peak delivery hours. Draft text is attached for your quick review and sign-off.
reply_tmpl: Approved. Send the final copy to Tina and we'll get it out on all channels today.

subject_tmpl: Safety advisory — scam targeting seniors in the village
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, our fraud unit has logged {NUM} reports in the past two weeks of a phone scam targeting older residents, in which callers impersonate the utility or a grandchild in distress and demand payment by gift card or wire. Reported losses so far total roughly {AMOUNT}. We'd like to issue a senior-focused safety advisory and partner with the township senior center on a short in-person briefing. The advisory urges residents never to pay by gift card and to call our non-emergency line at {PHONE} to verify. I've drafted the bulletin and a half-page handout for the senior center. Your endorsement in the message would help it land. Please review the attached draft.
reply_tmpl: Yes, let's get ahead of this. Add a line from me and coordinate the senior-center session with Kevin.

subject_tmpl: Monthly parking-citation revenue & top violation locations — {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the parking-enforcement summary for {DATE} is attached. Officers and the CSO issued {NUM} parking citations: overnight street-parking violations led at {NUM}, followed by expired-meter and permit-zone violations. Total parking-fine revenue posted was {AMOUNT}, about {NUM}% above last month. The top violation locations were the {ADDRESS} commuter lot, the {STREET} business district, and the blocks around the Metra station. We voided {NUM} citations on first-time-warning grounds. A few residents have asked about extending visitor-permit hours near {STREET}; I'll fold that into a separate recommendation if you want to explore it.
reply_tmpl: Thanks. Hold the visitor-permit idea for now, but send the revenue line to David for the monthly close.

subject_tmpl: FW: Constituent appeal of overnight-parking ticket — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, forwarding for your awareness an appeal from {FIRST} {LAST} of {ADDRESS} on an overnight-parking citation issued {DATE} (case {CASE}). They state they have a registered overnight permit and believe the ticket was issued in error. I checked the permit roll — their permit was active that night, so this looks like a legitimate void. I've already flagged the citation for dismissal at the administrative-hearing level and notified the resident at {PHONE} that no fine is owed. No action needed from your office; I just wanted you to have the resolution in hand in case they reached out to you as well.
reply_tmpl: Appreciate you catching that and closing the loop with them directly. Nothing further from me.

subject_tmpl: Snow-route parking ban enforcement summary — {DATE} event
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, here is the recap of enforcement during the snow-route parking ban for the {DATE} storm. Officers issued {NUM} warnings on the first pass and {NUM} citations on the second, and {NUM} vehicles were relocated by our towing contractor to clear the priority plow routes along {STREET} and {CROSS}. Public Works reported the routes cleared on schedule with no plow obstructions on the banned streets. Tow and relocation fees are documented in the impound log; affected owners were notified at the impound lot. Resident complaints were minimal — {NUM} total, mostly about signage visibility, which I've passed to Public Works. Full detail attached.

subject_tmpl: Recovered-property notice — stolen vehicle returned to owner, case {CASE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, a quick recovered-property note for the file. A vehicle reported stolen on {DATE} was located by patrol on {STREET} and confirmed via VIN as the stolen unit (case {CASE}). It was held briefly for forensic processing and has now been released to its registered owner, {FIRST} {LAST}, who was reached at {PHONE}. No suspects in custody yet; the case remains open with detectives. I'm noting it because recovered-property returns are a good-news item the board sometimes likes to hear about, and the owner specifically asked us to pass along their thanks to the department. Chain-of-custody documentation is complete.
reply_tmpl: Nice work by the patrol team — pass my thanks along. We can mention recoveries like this in the next newsletter.

subject_tmpl: Evidence/property & impound — recovered items pending owner pickup, {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the records unit completed its periodic recovered-property review for {DATE}. We currently hold {NUM} recovered or found-property items pending owner pickup, including {NUM} bicycles, several electronics, and recovered tools from a closed burglary case ({CASE}). We've sent owner-notification letters where we could identify a rightful owner; {NUM} items are unclaimed past the statutory hold and will go to the annual property auction if not retrieved. Impound-lot administrative fees collected this period totaled {AMOUNT}. A resident, {FIRST} {LAST}, asked about releasing an impounded vehicle and can be reached at {PHONE} — that's being handled at the records counter. Audit-ready logs are attached.

subject_tmpl: Warrant sweep results — {NUM} outstanding warrants cleared
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the multi-agency warrant sweep we ran on {DATE} wrapped up successfully. Working with the Cook County Sheriff's fugitive unit, officers cleared {NUM} outstanding warrants — {NUM} felony and {NUM} misdemeanor — including one subject wanted on a felony domestic-violence warrant who was apprehended without incident at {ADDRESS}. No injuries to officers or subjects, and no use of force above control holds. All arrestees were processed and transported to the Maybrook courthouse for bond court. LEADS entries were updated to reflect the cleared warrants. This kind of operation tends to draw a few resident questions, so I've prepared a short fact sheet you can use if anyone asks.
reply_tmpl: Excellent result, and good call on the fact sheet. Send it to Tina so we have consistent messaging if the press asks.

subject_tmpl: Monthly outstanding-warrant count for the board — {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the monthly outstanding-warrant count for the board packet is ready. As of {DATE} the village has {NUM} active local and bench warrants on file, down {NUM} from last month thanks to the recent sweep and several warrant clearances made during routine traffic stops. Of the active total, {NUM} are felony-level and the remainder are ordinance or failure-to-appear matters. All entries are current in LEADS. We served {NUM} warrants this month and {NUM} new ones were issued by the court. I've attached the count broken out by warrant type for the consent-agenda summary. Let me know if the board wants the trend chart added.
reply_tmpl: Add the trend chart — the board likes seeing the direction over time. Thanks.
